title: The costs of inflation
description: Practice what you've learned about the winners and losers from inflation and how inflation redistributes wealth in this exercise.
topic_path: Economic indicators and the business cycle

assessment_items.question_content:
**Who are the winners and losers from unexpected inflation?**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nobody wins from unanticipated inflation
Borrowers lose, lenders win
Both borrowers and lenders lose
Borrowers win, lenders lose
Both borrowers and lenders win

assessment_items.question_content:
A steady decline in the price level for many years indicates that Westeros has been experiencing deflation.

**Which of the following agents is most likely to be negatively impacted by the deflation in Westeros?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Iron Bank of Braavos, which has loaned $\$1$ million to the King interest free
The elderly citizens of Westeros, who receive $\$200$ every month as a pension
The King, who owes a lot of money to the Iron Bank
A girl who has stored $\$20$ in her mattress for $2$ years
Hodor, who has $\$500$ in a bank account earning $1\%$ interest every year

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following agents would benefit from unanticipated inflation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Bran, whose nominal income is the same every month
Grey Worm, who has $\$3{,}000$ in student loan debt that has a fixed interest rate of $3.5\%$
Olenna, who has stashed away money in a cave
Ygritte, who loaned Robb money last year at $3\%$
The soldiers of Westeros, who are paid wages based on the salary negotiated last year

assessment_items.question_content:
You borrow $\$200$ from the First Bank of Westeros to purchase Kraken repellant. The bank charges a fixed nominal interest rate of $18\%$ per year and you will repay them in one year. You and the bank both anticipate that there will be $7\%$ inflation. However, after the loan agreement is signed, the rate of inflation turns out to be $9\%$.

**Who is hurt by this unanticipated inflation, and why are they hurt by it?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The bank is hurt because the value of what is repaid has decreased
You are hurt because the value of what is repaid has decreased
The bank is hurt because the value of what is repaid has increased
You are hurt because the value of what is repaid has increased
We cannot tell without more information

assessment_items.question_content:
Melisandre asks you to loan her $\$600$ until next year. She offers to pay you $10\%$ annual interest. You are very suspicious of her motives and believe she is trying to gain at your expense. 

**What does Melisandre expect about the inflation rate if you know she is trying to benefit at your expense?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rate of inflation will be more than $10\%$
The rate of inflation will be less than $10\%$
There will be no inflation at all
There will be deflation
The rate of inflation will equal $10\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
Ygritte loaned Mance $\$900$. He agreed to repay her in full, plus $6\%$ interest, after one year. 

When he pays off his loan, Mance discovers he is better off than he had expected to be.

**Which of the following best describes what must have happened to the rate of inflation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Inflation was lower than he expected
Inflation was exactly what he expected
Inflation was higher than he expected
inflation is $4\%$, which he had expected
We cannot determine this based on the information given

assessment_items.question_content:
Cersei knows she will have to repay a debt of $\$100{,}000$ soon. She also anticipates there will be some inflation soon.

**Under which of the following circumstances will Cersei pay back the least amount of money in real terms?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is no inflation at all
The actual rate of inflation equals the expected rate of inflation
The actual rate of inflation is less than the expected rate of inflation
If the actual rate of inflation is greater than the expected rate of inflation
We cannot determine this without knowing what interest rate she is paying

assessment_items.question_content:
The Bank of King's Landing has made many loans to the lords of Winterfell. The Bank is considering whether they will benefit or not if the rate of inflation changed.

**Under which circumstance would the Bank realize an unexpected benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When the actual rate of inflation is equal to the anticipated rate of inflation
When the actual rate of inflation is higher than the anticipated rate of inflation
When the actual rate of inflation is lower than the anticipated rate of inflation
When the interest rate charged is equal to the expected rate of inflation
When the interest rate charged is lower than the actual rate of inflation.

assessment_items.question_content:
Tyrion has managed to save up $\$1{,}000$ which he has deposited in a Westeros Bank account that pays $4\%$ interest. 

**Which of the following will be true if the actual inflation rate is lower than the expected inflation rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Tyrion would benefit; the bank would be worse off
Tyrion and the bank would both benefit
Neither benefit
Both are worse off
We cannot tell without more information
